channel.title: The U-Curve Brain
channel.description: A macro-autonomous cognitive filter. Optimizing for agency, perspective, and resilience.

item.title: (Research) ‘Having poetry in a public space transports us, even if we don’t understand it’
item.description:
Source: Positive News

Forty years after its inception, the project of displaying poetry on London’s Underground continues to influence the daily commutes of millions. This initiative integrates art into public spaces, providing moments of cultural engagement for urban dwellers.

item.title: (Research) NewsFix - Clean Energy Hits Turning Point. 19.5 Million Lives Saved. Seagrass Comeback.
item.description:
Source: Fix The News

This NewsFix episode covers significant global developments: renewables now meet all new electricity demand, Africa's measles vaccination drive saved 19.5 million lives, a gene therapy restores hearing, poverty and mental health progress, expanded Chicago education, and seagrass ecosystem recovery. This collective progress enhances global stability and fosters a more sustainable and healthy future, indirectly benefiting UK/EU citizens through improved international relations and environmental health.

item.title: (Audio) Season 3 Episode 11 - BDSM and Unexpected Endings...
item.description:
Source: The Kink Perspective



This episode discusses the challenges of achieving closure after unexpected relationship endings, including those within BDSM contexts. It examines the emotional impact and provides strategies for healing, resilience, and compassion through reflective practices, acceptance, and forgiveness.

item.title: (Audio) January Series 2026 | Day 15 | Projection & Other People
item.description:
Source: The Sober Rebel



This podcast episode from Louisa Evans's 2026 January Series focuses on friendships in sobriety and the social shifts involved. It explores psychological projection and its manifestations when individuals choose to become sober, offering practical, psychology-led insights.

item.title: (Video) Semantics Standards and Tools for Spatial and Contextual Exposome Data, Shuteng Niu, March 13, 2026
item.description:
Source: NIEHS



Shuteng Niu, PhD (Mayo Clinic) and Xing He, PhD (Indiana University) present on semantics standards and tools for spatial and contextual exposome data. The discussion focuses on data management and interpretation in health research.

item.title: (Video) Get to Know the Student Flourishing Initiative at Center for Healthy Minds at UW–Madison
item.description:
Source: Healthy Minds



This video presents the Student Flourishing Initiative from the Center for Healthy Minds at UW–Madison. The program aims to enhance college students' wellbeing, drawing inspiration from the "Art and Science of Human Flourishing" framework.

item.title: (Video) 'physicists don't know how planes work'
item.description:
Source: Angela Collier



This video addresses the misconception that physicists lack understanding of airplane mechanics. It likely delves into the underlying scientific principles of aerodynamics, explaining how aircraft achieve flight despite popular misunderstandings.

item.title: (Video) The Science of Successful Workplaces | Greater Good Workplaces
item.description:
Source: Greater Good Science Center



This video introduces "Greater Good Workplaces," a program by the University of California, Berkeley, focusing on the scientific principles behind creating successful and positive work environments. It offers insights into transforming workplaces based on research.

item.title: (Video) Fast-Forward Your Life
item.description:
Source: Kurzgesagt – In a Nutshell



This video explores the hypothetical concept of fast-forwarding through undesirable life events. It examines the potential appeal of bypassing discomfort versus the valuable experiences and lessons that could be forfeited in such a process.

item.title: (Video) Shifting Light: Edinburgh voices on the future of higher education
item.description:
Source: The University of Edinburgh



Student journalists Luca Parrish and Amelie Jeeves interview Professor Sir Peter Mathis and others to discuss the evolving landscape and future challenges of higher education. The discussion features diverse perspectives from Edinburgh.

item.title: (Research) To be is to participate
item.description:
Source: Aeon | a world of ideas

This essay explores Lucien Lévy-Bruhl's philosophy that personhood is not inherent but emerges from a lifelong process of communal living, emphasizing "we" over "I." It challenges individualistic notions of self, proposing that identity is fundamentally shaped by participation with others.

item.title: (Adage) Dead men tell no tales (Release unchangeable past; focus on the present moment.)
item.description: A timeless principle for cognitive resilience.

item.title: (Reminder) Observe how many narratives thrive on fear and fault to control you, then choose to forge your own path with purpose.
